name: GitHub PR Feedback to Codex Revision Queue
purpose: Turn /codex revise PR comments into a tracked Codex revision request, move linked Plane work back to In Progress, and notify Slack.

security.recommended: Validate X-Hub-Signature-256 using the GitHub webhook secret stored in n8n credentials.

nodes.name: CONFIG
nodes.notes: First workflow node after the trigger. Store shared constants here instead of n8n global variables.

values.slack_revision_channel: Slack channel for revision queue messages

nodes.name: Codex revision requested
nodes.condition: comment is on a PR and starts with /codex revise

outputs.plane_issue_id: Plane issue UUID parsed from PR body or comment
outputs.plane_url: Plane task URL parsed from PR body or comment

nodes.name: Move Plane to In Progress
nodes.request: Use Plane API to set work item state to In Progress.
nodes.on_error: Continue to Slack and GitHub acknowledgement with a repair note.

nodes.name: Comment on Plane with revision request
nodes.request: Add Plane comment containing PR URL and revision request.
nodes.on_error: Continue to Slack and GitHub acknowledgement with a repair note.

nodes.name: Send revision request to Slack
nodes.on_error: Respond 502 so GitHub webhook delivery can retry.

nodes.name: Acknowledge revision on GitHub
nodes.on_error: Do not fail the revision queue if Slack and Plane already succeeded.

safe_failure_handling: Only respond to comments that begin with /codex revise.
Only process comments on pull requests, not ordinary issues.
If Plane cannot be resolved, still notify Slack and acknowledge GitHub.
Do not expose webhook secrets in Slack, Plane, or GitHub comments.
Keep Codex deployment out of this flow; this only queues revision work.
